title: X-tremely Fatty Venezuelan Plantain Empanadas
description: A rich and decadent Venezuelan-inspired empanada dish featuring coconut cream, steamed plantains, and a crispy pastry crust.
instructions: Peel and slice the plantains into 1-inch pieces.
Steam the plantain slices for 10 minutes, or until tender.
In a large bowl, combine flour, shortening, sugar, salt, eggs, and anise seeds. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of coconut cream in the center of each dough circle.
Top with steamed plantain slices and fold the dough in half to form a triangle.
Press the edges to seal the empanada, and use a fork to crimp the edges.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Brush with unsalted butter and sprinkle with palm sugar.
